instructions: Remove all usages of 'Playfair Display' (serif) and 'JetBrains Mono' (monospace) for headers/eyebrows.
The global sans-serif font (Geist) MUST be used for all headers, titles, and body texts to ensure a unified, modern mobile web app feel.
Ensure heading weights are semibold or bold (600/700) for clarity.
Do NOT use generic 'Inter', use 'Geist' or 'Manrope' or standard modern sans-serif.
Retain the existing warm cream palette (Aegis semantic palette).
Do NOT use completely transparent backgrounds for headers. Use solid or blurred backgrounds.

header_redesign: Mobile Header Redesign: Make the LargeTitle component look like a polished mobile app header (e.g., Apple iOS large titles or Material 3).
Remove the redundant 'Aegis vault' eyebrow text and line from all pages.
Use the actual page title (e.g., 'Your codes', 'Security') as the primary H1 header.
Ensure sticky headers have a backdrop blur (`backdrop-blur-xl` or similar) with a semi-transparent background to prevent content from bleeding through readability.

instructions_to_main_agent: In `LargeTitle`, completely remove the `div` containing the 'Aegis vault' eyebrow text and the horizontal line.
Ensure `h1` rendering the `title` prop looks large, bold, and uses the standard sans font.
Make sure the scrolling blur veil does not use a fully transparent background that breaks readability. Maintain the sticky scroll effect smoothly.
In `Display` component, remove `fontFamily: "'Playfair Display', serif"` from inline styles so it defaults to the modern sans font.
In `Eyebrow` component, remove `fontFamily: "'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace"` from inline styles so it matches the modern global font.
Remove `--font-serif` entirely from CSS variables to ensure the old font is completely deprecated.

accessibility: Ensure all text has adequate contrast ratios against its background.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Use `sonner` for toasts. Sonner component are located in `/app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx`.
